source: An Attentive Interaction Network for Context-aware Recommendations

concepts.finding: Attentive interaction network for context-aware recommendation. Models user-item interactions with: (1) feature-level attention (which features matter for this interaction), (2) instance-level attention (which historical interactions matter). Context (time, location, companion) modulates interaction weights. Outperforms FM and NFM on context-aware tasks.
concepts.implication: Feature-level attention identifies which keyword features (text, frequency, domain, entity type) matter most for intent prediction. Instance-level attention selects relevant historical keyword-intent examples for the current keyword. Context modulates intent assignment.
concepts.open_question: What contextual features (time, device, domain, season) most strongly influence keyword intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: An attentive interaction network for keyword-intent matching outperforms flat embedding similarity for context-dependent keywords (e.g., seasonal, domain-specific).
